purpose: Keep Bears agentic workflow small, parallel, measurable, file-backed, role-scoped, and reusable across plugin, platform, gitops, infra, and product_infra work.

principles.rule: Documentation lives in the repository beside governed logic and is checked by validators.
Durable packet work starts from schema inputs, outputs, required fields, and failure modes before implementation.
State packets, logic packets, scope rows, decision logs, and degradation packets must have JSON Schema coverage.
Do not duplicate workflow logic across roles, hooks, catalogs, and docs; keep one authoritative source and references.
Executable workflow logic is split into small scripts, functions, schemas, fixtures, metrics, and docs.
Each module, role, and packet owns one checkable responsibility.
Reusable logic is packaged as schema, validator, fixtures, metrics, docs, and a stable command.
Agents depend on packet inputs and outputs, not hidden implementation details.
Workflow behavior is composed from small policies and packets, not deep role inheritance chains.
A durable workflow rule is incomplete until CI can run a deterministic validate command for it.
Each agent layer receives only the smallest packet set needed for its responsibility.
Important decisions, scopes, tasks, metrics, blockers, and status live in JSON files, not only chat.
Any decision that changes agent behavior must be present in a file, schema, fixture, metric, doc, or test.
The same packet input must produce the same validation decision.
Workflow commands must be safe to rerun without corrupting state.
Good and bad packets must exist for durable workflow logic.
A scope or task is incomplete without time, token, error, heartbeat, progress, and blocker metrics when applicable.
Logic changes name the docs, catalogs, roles, schemas, and fixtures that must change with them.
An agent may edit a governed file only when its role packet permits that file class and write scope.
Every scope starts by selecting one repo domain before task execution.
Missing role, schema, metrics, owner, budget, or allowed write scope blocks the affected scope.
Schemas and packets carry schema_version or version and require explicit migration steps for incompatible changes.
New validators must not break old valid packets unless a migration packet exists.
Every durable workflow rule has one owning catalog or schema.
Access, deploy, runtime, role, and security policies are checked by files and commands.
Metrics, alerts, dashboards, and compact summaries are repo-described and not free-form chat state.
A logic package solves one task and can be reused by another workflow.
There is one recommended launch path and it is always validated.
Architecture-impacting changes write a short decision record with decision, reason, and changed surface.
